meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Learn how to build user onboarding and overcome tutorial hell. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: To escape tutorial hell, stop watching and start building a functional user onboarding flow. By integrating a concrete feature like a sign-up walkthrough, you shift from passive consumption to active engineering. Use Antigravity to scaffold the logic and Claude Code to implement the state management, transforming theory into a portfolio-ready asset.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hundreds of hours watching polished videos, typing out code that works perfectly in the tutorial but breaks the moment you blink. You are trapped in the 'Tutorial Hell' loop—a place where you know the syntax but lack the confidence to build something of your own. It is time to stop being a spectator and start being an architect.

Building a user onboarding flow is the perfect antidote. It forces you to handle real-world states, user input, and data persistence. By leveraging modern AI tooling like Antigravity and Claude Code, you will move from 'copy-paste' to 'problem-solving,' building a project that actually demonstrates your capability to future employers.

steps.title: Define the Onboarding Logic
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the user journey. We will focus on a three-step modal sequence that captures user intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a 3-step user onboarding process. Include fields for user name, interest area, and experience level. Define the state management logic for moving between steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate onboarding schema and state machine logic for react'

steps.title: Scaffold the Interface
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the UI components based on your schema. This keeps your code modular and clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate React components for the onboarding flow using Tailwind CSS. Ensure each step is a separate functional component with props for data handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'generate onboarding UI components with Tailwind'

steps.title: Implement State Persistence
steps.description: Use Claude Code to connect your UI to a local state manager so the onboarding progress is saved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a hook to manage the onboarding state and persist it to localStorage when the user clicks 'next'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'implement useOnboarding hook with localStorage persistence'

steps.title: Add Validation Logic
steps.description: Ensure users cannot skip mandatory steps. This adds the professional polish that separates a student project from a production app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add Zod validation for the onboarding inputs to ensure the user name is not empty and experience level is selected.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix 'add Zod validation to onboarding form fields'

steps.title: Final Integration and Testing
steps.description: Review the full flow and ensure the transition between steps is seamless and error-free.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the entire onboarding flow for potential race conditions or state bugs. Suggest improvements for performance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'run end-to-end test on onboarding flow'

pro_tips: Always build your state management before the UI; it prevents the 'spaghetti code' trap.
Use Zod for runtime validation; it catches errors before they reach your database.
Implement a 'skip' button for power users; it demonstrates you understand user experience.
Focus on one single feature per session; completion momentum is your best friend in avoiding burnout.
Document your 'why' in the README; recruiters care more about your decisions than the code itself.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, try adding an email capture integration using a service like Resend or Mailchimp to see how your app handles external APIs.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-ready feature in your portfolio. Don't stop here; iterate, deploy, and show the world what you've built. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
